blockingIssues.description: DesignerTaskList omits the valid `cancelled` ReviewTask status from its filter model, so the UI cannot represent all initiated tasks as required by VAL-TRACK-001/002.
ResubmissionTaskList filters only `status === 'rejected'`, which hides returned draft tasks that remain the active resubmission records in the workflow.
TaskReviewDetail bundles the `再次提交` action into the detail-modal feature even though this feature contract is display-only, coupling unrelated behavior into the milestone review surface.
After successful resubmission, TaskReviewDetail keeps rendering the stale rejected task object, so the modal does not reflect the required submitted status update.
The reviewed change only adds a narrow store-level regression test and does not implement or verify the full initiator → reviewer → submit integration flow promised by VAL-CROSS-001.
Returned-task logic is internally inconsistent: useUserStore maps returned design-node tasks to `rejected`, while reviewTaskFilters still recognizes returned tasks as `draft + returnReason`.
WebSocket task updates are stored without REST-style normalization, so snake_case payloads can break ownership/status filters and prevent automatic list refreshes.
The cited commit/handoff evidence does not implement navigation-state persistence for reviewer task lists; it points to an unrelated websocket commit instead.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten UI/integration worker skills so milestone features that fulfill user-visible assertions cannot be completed without feature-appropriate interactive verification evidence, and clarify when user-testing validators may substitute for worker-side manual checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review reports for m5-designer-task-list-ui, cross-complete-flow-integration, and cross-websocket-notification all found workers completed user-facing or cross-role features without the manual/browser verification required by their skills.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for workers operating in dirty workspaces to ensure handoff `commitId` values reference the actual feature implementation commit(s), or explicitly document when no reliable per-feature commit exists.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review reports for cross-complete-flow-integration and cross-navigation-state-persistence found handoffs pointing at unrelated commits, making diff-based review unreliable.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the current 'task detail page' wording in shared notes as-is.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; the review surfaced a naming inconsistency, but this is a normative documentation decision for the orchestrator rather than a factual shared-state update to apply directly.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a library note that returned tasks may be either `rejected` or `draft + returnReason`.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the competing contracts are exactly the blocking inconsistency found in this milestone; codifying both states as accepted knowledge would cement incorrect ambiguity instead of resolving it.
